title: storage — kill switches
description: Emergency operational switches. Flipping is instantaneous (no deploy). Turning off any of these is a runbook step logged in the incident retro.

flags.description: Master switch for the upload surface. Off = every POST to /files or /files/chunked returns HTTP 503 STORAGE_UPLOADS_DISABLED. Use during S3 backend outage or bucket-key rotation.
Master switch for the redemption surface. Off = /files/{signature} returns HTTP 503. Downstream: images and attachments fail to render across every SDUI screen. Emergency-only.
GenerateFileVariantsJob execution. Off = variants queued but not run (uploads still succeed; existing variants still serve).
ScanFileForVirusesJob execution. Off = files skip scan and land as virus_scan_state=`scan_skipped`. NEVER turn off in production without a compliance approval + retro.
Chunked upload surface. Off = clients must fall back to single-request uploads for files under the direct-upload ceiling; larger files fail.
Content-addressable dedup. Off = every upload creates a fresh blob even for matching SHA-256. Emergency-only for cases where refcount drift is suspected.
Signed URL issuance. Off = every read fails. Even more emergency-critical than downloads_enabled.
SignedUrlAudit hit_count increment. Off = downloads still serve but audit trail loses per-hit fidelity. Recovery mode during audit-table lock contention.
PurgeExpiredFilesJob execution. Off = expired files linger. Emergency-only during regulatory investigations that pause purges.
storage.quota.enforce middleware. Off = uploads bypass quota check (fail-open on overquota). Aligns with entitlements.graceful_degradation_mode; auto-disable after 60 minutes.
